# dockPerspective.v1 vs shipped dockLayout.v2: reconcile the schema naming

## Context
A naming seam between design authority and shipped substrate, surfaced by the fable budget-regime planning audit (2026-07-04): ADR 0029 §2.2 names a NEW persisted schema `neo.harness.dockPerspective.v1`; the perspective substrate that actually landed (`#14651`/`#14652`/`#14667`, merged) ships `neo.harness.dockLayout.v2` (capture-scope + fingerprint + perspective fields on the existing envelope family) + `dockLayoutCollection.v1`. Two names, one capability territory. Filed to the delegatable bar: **non-fable implementable** — it is a decision-then-docs leaf with a bounded code option.

## The Problem
Future leaves (`#14653`/`#14668` restore, `#14669` store, `#14616` presets, `#14649` NL tools) will cite one name or the other; unreconciled, the substrate accumulates dual vocabulary and a future agent burns a session proving they are (or are not) the same thing. The ADR's anti-anchor already bans "a third collection shape" — the reconciliation must not mint one.

## The Architectural Reality
- Shipped truth: `DockZoneModel.LAYOUT_SCHEMA = 'neo.harness.dockLayout.v2'` (+ `LAYOUT_SCHEMA_V1` read-path, `LAYOUT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA`), with `captureScope: 'window'|'topology'`, `windowFingerprint`, `perspectiveName`, `windowDocuments` — functionally covering §2.2's capture scopes.
- ADR posture: amend-this-first (a leaf that contradicts a section amends the ADR in its own reviewed change, ADR-0005 lifecycle).
- Two clean resolutions: **(a) amend ADR 0029 §2.2** to name the shipped `dockLayout.v2` envelope as the perspective carrier (v1 of the CAPABILITY, v2 of the ENVELOPE — one sentence + table row edit), or **(b)** ship a thin `dockPerspective.v1` alias/wrapper — REJECTED-by-default: it is the "third collection shape" the ADR's own anti-anchor forbids unless a falsifier shows the envelope cannot carry a §2.2 obligation.

## The Fix
One PR, option (a) unless falsified during intake: amend ADR 0029 §2.2 naming (ADR-0005 lifecycle, cross-family review), sweep the open dock tickets' prose for the stale name, and add the schema-name row to `HarnessDockZoneModel.md`'s persistence section so both tiers agree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One canonical schema name for perspectives across ADR 0029, the model contract doc, and open ticket prose.
- [ ] The anti-anchor holds: no third collection shape introduced.
- [ ] ADR amendment follows the ADR-0005 lifecycle (its own reviewed change, cross-family).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
Restore semantics (`#14653`/`#14668`) · the store leaf (`#14669`) · any envelope field changes.

## Related
Parent `#13158` · authority ADR 0029 §2.2 + ADR 0005 lifecycle · shipped substrate `#14651`/`#14652`/`#14667`. Decision Record impact: **amends ADR 0029** (naming only).

## Ticket-intake classification — needs-contract-alignment / not-code-ready

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT] Intake verdict: **needs-relinking / needs-contract-alignment before implementation**. The ticket premise is real, but the body is not yet code-ready because it proposes an ADR + contract-doc + open-ticket naming reconciliation without the required Contract Ledger matrix for the affected surfaces.

Evidence checked 2026-07-04:

- Live ticket #14773 is unassigned, created/updated `2026-07-04T13:17:32Z`, labels `documentation`, `enhancement`, `ai`, `architecture`; bot-state band is **pre-stale** under `.github/workflows/close-inactive-issues.yml` (`90` stale days + `14` close days), with no `stale` / `no auto close` label.
- Parent epic #13158 has an existing @neo-gpt epic-review comment, so the epic prerequisite is satisfied.
- Current source confirms the seam: ADR 0029 §2.2 still names `neo.harness.dockPerspective.v1`, while `src/dashboard/DockZoneModel.mjs` now writes `DockZoneModel.LAYOUT_SCHEMA = 'neo.harness.dockLayout.v2'` with perspective fields plus `LAYOUT_SCHEMA_V1` and `LAYOUT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA` read/collection semantics.
- `learn/agentos/HarnessDockZoneModel.md` still shows named perspectives examples with `neo.harness.dockLayoutCollection.v1` containing `neo.harness.dockLayout.v1`, so the doc surface also needs an explicit disposition.
- Duplicate/successor sweep: KB connected #14773 to #13158 / ADR 0029 / shipped perspective leaves; `gh issue list --search "dockPerspective dockLayout"` returned only #14773; `gh pr list --search "dockPerspective dockLayout"` and `gh pr list --search "14773"` returned no resolving PR.
- ADR successor-risk: **adr-amendment-required** — this lane amends ADR 0029 naming, and must not silently bypass the ADR or mint the third collection shape ADR 0029 explicitly forbids.

Blocking readiness gap:

Ticket-intake §7 requires a Contract Ledger when a ticket modifies a surface consumed by humans/agents or names existing fields/docs/runtime surfaces. This ticket names at least these public/contract surfaces, but has no matrix:

| Target Surface | Current Source of Authority | Required disposition before branch work |
|---|---|---|
| ADR 0029 §2.2 wrapper row + JSON example | `learn/agentos/decisions/0029-harness-docking-design.md` | Decide whether ADR text amends to shipped `dockLayout.v2` as the perspective carrier, or explicitly preserves a future `dockPerspective.v1` shape with evidence. |
| Runtime schema constants | `src/dashboard/DockZoneModel.mjs` `LAYOUT_SCHEMA`, `LAYOUT_SCHEMA_V1`, `LAYOUT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA` | State whether code remains unchanged and docs follow shipped `dockLayout.v2`, or what code change would be required. |
| Model contract doc persistence examples | `learn/agentos/HarnessDockZoneModel.md` named-layout/persistence sections | Name the exact example/schema rows to update so guide and runtime agree. |
| Open dock follow-up ticket prose | #14653 / #14668 / #14669 / #14616 / #14649 or current surviving successors | Sweep and list which tickets carry stale `dockPerspective.v1` wording vs already use shipped `dockLayout.v2`. |
| Anti-anchor | ADR 0029 "no third collection shape" | Preserve as an explicit no-new-envelope constraint unless falsified by runtime evidence. |

Routing: I added `not-code-ready` + `needs-re-triage` so this drops from implementation pickup until the contract ledger is added or the ticket is narrowed to a purely documented ADR amendment with the above surfaces enumerated.
